title: Alter column type via drop/re-add pattern
prompt: An existing products table has `price :float`. Write a migration that converts it to `:decimal`. Since alter modify on a float→decimal conversion can fail silently in Postgres for some values, use the safe pattern: add a new column `price_decimal`, execute/1 a backfill (`execute "UPDATE products SET price_decimal = price::decimal"`), then in a later migration rename. For this challenge, just write the first migration that adds `price_decimal` and backfills. Write to priv/repo/migrations/20260412800000_add_price_decimal_to_products.exs.
tier_rationale: Requires the add-column-then-backfill pattern rather than in-place modify. Sonnet often just writes modify.
research_citation: Hashrocket — Ecto Migrations: Simple to Complex.
